ail: verb. To cause to suffer; to trouble, afflict. (Now chiefly in interrogative or indefinite constructions.)
ait: noun. An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.
alit: verb. simple past and past participle of alight
alt: noun. Of a voice or instrument, high pitch; especially, the octave above the top line of the treble stave.
avid: adjective. Enthusiastic; keen; eager; showing great interest in something or desire to do something.
avidly: adverb. In an avid manner; greedily; eagerly.
daily: adjective. That occurs or attends every day, or at least every working day.
dal: noun. Any of many dried husked pulses (legume), including peas, beans and lentils.

davy: noun. An affidavit, a legally binding statement or oath
day: noun. The time when the Sun is above the horizon and it lights the sky.
daylit: verb. simple past and past participle of daylight
dial: noun. A graduated, circular scale over which a needle moves to show a measurement (such as speed).
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
diva: noun. Any female celebrity, usually a well known singer or actress.
idly: adverb. Without specific purpose, intent or effort.
ivy: noun. Any of several woody, climbing, or trailing evergreen plants of the genus Hedera.
lad: noun. A boy or young man.
lady: noun. The mistress of a household.
laid: verb. simple past and past participle of lay
laity: noun. People of a church who are not ordained clergy or clerics.
lat: noun. A staff, particularly one of an Indian kind.
lay: verb. To place down in a position of rest, or in a horizontal position.
lid: noun. The top or cover of a container.
lit: verb. simple past and past participle of light (“illuminate; start a fire; etc”)
livid: adjective. Having a dark, bluish appearance.
tad: noun. A small amount; a little bit.
tail: noun. The caudal appendage of an animal that is attached to their posterior and near the anus or cloaca.
tav: noun. Alternative form of taw (Semitic letter)
tidily: adverb. In a tidy manner; neatly; cleanly.
tidy: adjective. Arranged neatly and in order.
validity: noun. The state of being valid, authentic or genuine.
vat: noun. A large tub, such as is used for making wine or for tanning.
via: noun. A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
vial: noun. A glass or plastic vessel or bottle, especially a small tube-shaped bottle used to store medicine, perfume or other chemicals.
vita: noun. A hagiography; a biography of a saint.
vital: adjective. Relating to or characteristic of life.
yid: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Yid.
ady: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for West Circassian.
dali: noun. A timber tree (genus Myristica) of Guiana, whose wood is used for staves etc.
datil: noun. A datil pepper, a very spicy pepper of the species Capsicum chinense.
dital: noun. A finger -operated key for raising the pitch of a guitar by a semitone.
divi: noun. The dividend paid out by the Co-op
ida: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Idakho-Isukha-Tiriki.
itd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Tidung.
lai: noun. A mostly North European medieval form of lyrical, narrative poem written in octosyllabic couplets that often deals with tales of
lait: verb. To seek; search for; inquire.
tai: noun. A fish, the Japanese bream.
tilia: noun. Any tree of the linden genus Tilia.
vii: symbol. Ⅶ, the Roman numeral seven (7).
vila: noun. A type of female nature spirit in Slavic mythology, similar in some ways to a fairy or nymph.
vility: noun. The quality of being vile or base.
vly: noun. A small swampy or marshy area, especially near a stream.
yad: noun. A pointer used in the ritual of Torah -reading.
yali: noun. A type of house, particularly found on the Bosporus.
yat: noun. A vowel letter of the Cyrillic and Glagolitic alphabet (Cyrillic capital Ѣ, Cyrillic small ѣ, Glagolitic ⱑ), no longer in current use
yilt: noun. Alternative form of gilt (“young female pig, at or nearing the age of first breeding”).
